text:
To determine how many people were in the church next door, we start by calculating the total number of cookies brought to the reception. Given that there were 100 guests and each brought a plate of 15 cookies, the total number of cookies is calculated as follows:
\[ 100 \text{ guests} \times 15 \text{ cookies/guest} = 1500 \text{ cookies} \]

The bride gave half of these cookies to the church next door. Therefore, the number of cookies given to the church is:
\[ \frac{1500 \text{ cookies}}{2} = 750 \text{ cookies} \]

If each person in the church next door received 15 cookies, the number of people in the church can be found by dividing the total number of cookies given to the church by the number of cookies each person received:
\[ \frac{750 \text{ cookies}}{15 \text{ cookies/person}} = 50 \text{ people} \]

Thus, the number of people in the church next door is \(\boxed{50}\).
